slots.title: Deposit flight is a pricing problem, not a loyalty problem
slots.subtitle: Findings and recommendation from the eight-week deposit strategy review

slots.headline: Repricing the top decile of balances retains EUR 3.1B at a quarter of the blanket-rate cost.
slots.bullet-1: Meridianbank lost EUR 4.2B in deposits over 12 months; 78% of the outflow came from 6% of accounts.
slots.bullet-2: Exit interviews show rate, not service, drove 9 of 10 large withdrawals; median gap to the winning offer was 85bps.
slots.bullet-3: A blanket rate match would cost EUR 96M per year; a targeted top-decile reprice costs EUR 24M for 74% of the retention effect.
slots.bullet-4: The branch network flags at-risk balances 6 weeks late; the data to flag them at week zero already exists in core banking.
slots.bullet-5: We recommend a two-tier reprice plus an early-warning trigger, piloted in the Northeast region in Q3.
slots.source: Meridianbank core banking extract, Jan 2025 to Apr 2026; 214 exit interviews

slots.section-blurb: Outflow concentrated in three competitor money-market offers, all launched within the same five-month window.

slots.headline: The outflow is concentrated, which makes it addressable
slots.number-label: of outflow came from 6% of accounts
slots.context-1: 2,140 accounts above EUR 250K drove EUR 3.3B of the EUR 4.2B total outflow.
slots.context-2: These accounts hold an average of 2.7 products; they are profitable beyond the deposit relationship.
slots.context-3: Their rate sensitivity is binary: balances move in full, not in increments, once a threshold gap opens.
slots.source: Core banking extract; account-level flows Jan 2025 to Apr 2026

slots.headline: Three forces explain the timing of the outflow
slots.col-body-1: Three competitor money-market products launched between May and September 2025, each paying 85 to 120bps above our flagship savings rate.
slots.col-body-2: Instant account portability went live in April 2025; median switch time for affluent clients fell from 9 days to 40 minutes.
slots.col-body-3: EUR 1.9B of promotional term deposits matured in Q3 2025 with no successor offer in market, releasing balances at the worst moment.
slots.source: Competitor rate sheets; ECB instant-payments adoption data; internal maturity ladder

slots.headline: The retention play runs in five moves over one quarter
slots.step-body-1: Early-warning trigger on balance velocity and rate-shopping signals in core banking. Flags fire the moment a balance moves, not six weeks later in the branch report.
slots.step-body-2: Score flagged accounts by relationship profitability, not balance alone. An account with three products and a mortgage is worth a different offer than a rate tourist.
slots.step-body-3: Top decile gets the matched rate; second decile gets the loyalty step-up. Everyone else keeps the book rate, which caps the cost of the program.
slots.step-body-4: Relationship managers call within 48 hours of a flag, with the offer pre-approved. No escalation loop; the playbook decides, the RM delivers.
slots.step-body-5: Weekly retention cohorts against the locked holdout group. Kill rules are predefined: two consecutive weeks below threshold ends the pilot.
slots.source: Pilot design as agreed with Retail Banking ExCo, 28 May 2026

slots.headline: Targeted reprice beats both alternatives on cost per euro retained
slots.row-headers: Annual cost | Deposits retained | Cost per euro retained | Margin impact | Time to launch
slots.col-headers: Do nothing | Blanket rate match | Targeted two-tier reprice
slots.cells: EUR 0 / EUR 96M / EUR 24M || EUR 0 / EUR 4.2B / EUR 3.1B || n.a. / 2.3 cents / 0.8 cents || baseline / minus 11bps NIM / minus 3bps NIM || n.a. / 2 weeks / 6 weeks
slots.source: Team analysis; retention elasticities from 2024 rate-move natural experiment

slots.call-to-action: Approve the Northeast pilot by 15 July to catch the Q3 maturity wall
slots.next-steps:
Week 1: trigger logic into the core banking sandbox
Week 2 to 3: RM playbook and offer approval matrix
Week 4: pilot live in 40 branches, holdout group locked
Week 13: ExCo decision on national rollout
